title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Configure the computational environment with all required libraries for CAGER experiments including PyTorch, TransformerLens, and SAE training utilities.

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.1+ with CUDA 12.1: pip install torch torchvision torchaudio --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step3: Install TransformerLens: pip install transformer-lens>=1.14.0
steps.step4: Install HuggingFace ecosystem: pip install transformers datasets accelerate>=0.25.0
steps.step5: Install scientific computing: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn pandas matplotlib seaborn
steps.step6: Install einops and fancy-einsum for efficient tensor operations: pip install einops fancy-einsum
steps.step7: Install wandb for experiment tracking (optional): pip install wandb
steps.step8: Verify CUDA availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.is_available(), torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step9: Set random seeds for reproducibility: seeds = [42, 123, 456] for all experiments

title: Create Project Directory Structure
description: Set up organized directory structure for code, data, models, results, and figures.

steps.step1: Create directory structure: mkdir -p src data models results figures logs checkpoints
steps.step2: Create subdirectories for each experiment type: mkdir -p results/{synthetic,ioi,ravel} figures/{synthetic,ioi,ravel}
steps.step3: Create __init__.py files for Python packages: touch src/__init__.py
steps.step4: Set environment variable for project root: export PROJECT_ROOT=$(pwd)

title: Generate Synthetic Ground-Truth Dataset
description: Create synthetic MLP with known causal features for validating C-GAS metric. This provides ground truth to verify that C-GAS correlates with true feature recovery.

steps.step1: Define 5 known ground-truth causal features: f1(x)=sin(2πx1), f2(x)=relu(x2-0.5), f3(x)=x3*x4, f4(x)=exp(-x5^2), f5(x)=sign(x6*x7)
steps.step2: Generate 10,000 training samples with 20 input dimensions (5 causal + 15 noise) using seed=42
steps.step3: Create MLP architecture: input(20) -> hidden(64) -> hidden(64) -> output(1)
steps.step4: Train MLP to compute output = Σ wi * fi(x) + 0.1*noise where weights wi = [1.0, 0.8, -0.5, 0.6, -0.3]
steps.step5: Train for 100 epochs with Adam (lr=1e-3), batch_size=128, MSE loss
steps.step7: Generate evaluation set: 1,000 samples with seed=123 for validation experiments
steps.step8: Save ground-truth feature activations for all samples to data/synthetic_ground_truth.h5

title: Prepare Indirect Object Identification (IOI) Dataset
description: Load and preprocess IOI dataset with contrastive pairs for activation patching. IOI is a well-understood circuit task for sanity checking.

steps.step1: Load IOI dataset using baukit or custom implementation (~500 template pairs)
steps.step2: Create templates: 'When [A] and [B] went to the store, [A] gave a drink to [B]' vs 'When [A] and [B] went to the store, [B] gave a drink to [A]'
steps.step3: Extract 200 sentence pairs with names from diverse set (50% male, 50% female names)
steps.step4: Tokenize with GPT-2 tokenizer: pad/truncate to max_length=50
steps.step5: Identify token positions: [IO] (indirect object position), [S] (subject position), [END] token
steps.step6: Save processed dataset to data/ioi_dataset.pt with fields: templates, tokenized, positions, labels
steps.step7: Create train/val split: 150 train / 50 val with seed=42

title: Prepare RAVEL Factual Recall Dataset
description: Load RAVEL dataset for evaluating attribute disentanglement and factual recall tasks.

steps.step1: Download RAVEL dataset from HuggingFace datasets or use manual download
steps.step2: Filter for entity-attribute pairs with clear causal relationships: country-capital, profession-gender, etc.
steps.step3: Select 3 attribute types: (1) country-capital, (2) name-occupation, (3) company-CEO
steps.step4: Extract 300 entity pairs (100 per attribute type)
steps.step5: Create contrastive pairs for each entity: [entity] is from [country1] vs [entity] is from [country2]
steps.step6: Tokenize with GPT-2 tokenizer: truncate to max_length=40
steps.step7: Save to data/ravel_dataset.pt with structure: {entity, attribute_type, base_prompt, contrast_prompt, target_token_idx}
steps.step8: Create train/test split: 240 train / 60 test with seed=42

title: Train Baseline Interpretability Methods - Random Projections
description: Simple baseline using random projections to establish lower bound on C-GAS scores.

steps.step1: For each dataset, extract hidden activations from target layer (layer 6 for GPT-2, layer 4 for Pythia)
steps.step2: Generate random projection matrix with same dimensionality as target SAE (1x, 4x, 16x overcomplete)
steps.step3: Normalize activations to unit norm before projection
steps.step4: For each random seed [42, 123, 456]: generate new random matrix and save projections
steps.step5: Save baseline features to models/baselines/random_{dataset}_{seed}.pt

estimated_time: 10 minutes per dataset × 3 seeds = 30 minutes total

title: Train Baseline Interpretability Methods - PCA
description: Stronger linear baseline using Principal Component Analysis for dimensionality reduction.

steps.step1: Extract activations from training split for each dataset (~10,000 samples)
steps.step2: Fit PCA with varying number of components: k=64 (1x), k=256 (4x), k=1024 (16x) for hidden_dim=64
steps.step3: Compute explained variance ratio for each component configuration
steps.step4: Transform both train and validation activations using fitted PCA
steps.step5: Save PCA models and transformed features: models/baselines/pca_{dataset}_{k}components.pt
steps.step6: Run for each random seed [42, 123, 456] to measure variance from data sampling

estimated_time: 15 minutes per dataset × 3 seeds = 45 minutes total

title: Train Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) - Synthetic Task
description: Train SAEs on synthetic MLP hidden activations with varying dictionary sizes.

steps.step1: Extract hidden activations from layer 1 (64-dim) of synthetic MLP for all training samples
steps.step2: SAE architecture: encoder(64 -> dict_size), decoder(dict_size -> 64), ReLU activation
steps.step3: Train SAE with dictionary sizes: 64 (1x), 256 (4x), 1024 (16x) overcomplete
steps.step4: Training config: lr=1e-3, batch_size=256, L1 penalty=1e-4, 500 epochs
steps.step5: Loss: reconstruction_MSE + L1_sparsity
steps.step6: Track metrics: reconstruction error, sparsity (% zeros), dead neurons

estimated_time: 1 hour per dictionary size × 3 sizes × 3 seeds = 3 hours (can parallelize)

title: Train Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) - IOI Task
description: Train SAEs on GPT-2 Small activations for IOI circuit layers.

steps.step1: Load GPT-2 Small (124M) with TransformerLens: HookedTransformer.from_pretrained('gpt2-small')
steps.step2: Extract activations from layer 9 (known IOI circuit location, d_model=768)
steps.step3: SAE architecture: encoder(768 -> dict_size), decoder(dict_size -> 768)
steps.step4: Train with dictionary sizes: 768 (1x), 3072 (4x), 12288 (16x)
steps.step5: Training config: lr=5e-4, batch_size=64, L1 penalty=5e-5, 300 epochs (early stopping at 10 epochs no improvement)
steps.step6: Track: reconstruction loss, L0 sparsity (avg active features per sample), dead neurons (<1% activation frequency)

estimated_time: 1.5 hours per dictionary size × 3 sizes × 3 seeds = 4.5 hours (can parallelize)

title: Train Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) - RAVEL Task
description: Train SAEs on GPT-2 activations for RAVEL factual recall task.

steps.step1: Extract activations from layer 8-10 of GPT-2 Small (factual recall typically in mid-late layers)
steps.step2: Use same SAE architecture as IOI: encoder(768 -> dict_size), decoder(dict_size -> 768)
steps.step3: Train with dictionary sizes: 768 (1x), 3072 (4x), 12288 (16x)
steps.step4: Training config: lr=5e-4, batch_size=64, L1 penalty=5e-5, 300 epochs
steps.step5: Track same metrics as IOI SAEs

estimated_time: 1.5 hours per dictionary size × 3 sizes × 3 seeds = 4.5 hours (can parallelize)

title: Causal Subspace Identification - Synthetic Task
description: Use activation patching to identify candidate causal subspaces in synthetic MLP.

steps.step1: For each of 5 ground-truth features, create contrastive input pairs differing only in that feature
steps.step2: Run activation patching: patch activations from input A to input B at each hidden dimension
steps.step3: Measure causal effect: |output_patched - output_original| for each dimension
steps.step4: Identify top-k dimensions with largest causal effects for each feature (k=5, 10, 20)
steps.step5: Record candidate subspaces and their effect magnitudes

title: Causal Subspace Identification - IOI Task
description: Use activation patching to identify causal subspaces for IOI circuit components.

steps.step1: Focus on layers 8-10 of GPT-2 Small where IOI circuit operates
steps.step2: Create contrastive pairs: (S, IO) sentences with different indirect objects
steps.step3: Run activation patching at each layer for attention heads and MLP outputs
steps.step4: For each token position [IO], [S], [END], patch activations and measure change in prediction probability for correct indirect object
steps.step5: Identify subspaces where patching changes prediction probability by >10%
steps.step6: Record top 50 dimensions per layer with highest causal effects

title: Causal Subspace Identification - RAVEL Task
description: Identify causal subspaces for factual recall in RAVEL dataset.

steps.step1: For each attribute type (country-capital, name-occupation, company-CEO), extract contrastive pairs
steps.step2: Run activation patching at layers 8-10 of GPT-2
steps.step3: Measure change in target attribute prediction probability
steps.step4: Identify subspaces where patching produces >15% change in target probability
steps.step5: Aggregate across all entities within each attribute type
steps.step6: Record top 100 dimensions per attribute type

title: Multi-Method Validation - Pathway Consistency Check
description: First validation method: verify patching effects are consistent across different source inputs.

steps.step1: For each candidate subspace from activation patching, test with 10 different source inputs sharing same attribute value
steps.step2: Compute effect variance across sources: σ² = Var(effect_i across i=1..10)
steps.step3: If σ² > threshold (0.1 * mean_effect), flag as potential illusion (inconsistent pathway activation)
steps.step4: Pass check if σ² <= threshold for at least 80% of source input groups
steps.step5: Record results: results/{dataset}/validation_pathway_consistency.json

title: Multi-Method Validation - Ablation Consistency Check
description: Second validation method: compare activation patching with direct ablation effects.

steps.step1: For each candidate subspace, compute effect via direct ablation (zero out subspace)
steps.step2: Compare ablation effect with patching effect: relative_diff = |effect_patch - effect_ablation| / |effect_patch|
steps.step3: Pass check if relative_diff < 0.5 (effects within 50% of each other)
steps.step4: High relative difference indicates pathway-dependent effects (illusion indicator)
steps.step5: Record results: results/{dataset}/validation_ablation_consistency.json

title: Multi-Method Validation - Gradient Agreement Check
description: Third validation method: verify gradient directions align with intervention effects.

steps.step1: Compute gradients of target output w.r.t. each candidate subspace: ∇_subspace L
steps.step2: Compute expected intervention direction from gradient sign
steps.step3: Check if intervention effect direction matches gradient direction (cosine similarity > 0)
steps.step4: Pass check if gradient and intervention agree on direction for >70% of subspaces
steps.step5: Misalignment suggests non-gradient pathways (illusion indicator)

title: Construct Validated Causal Subspace Atlas
description: Aggregate validation results and construct final validated causal subspace atlas.

steps.step1: Load all three validation results for each dataset
steps.step2: Accept subspace into atlas if it passes >= 2 of 3 validation checks
steps.step3: For synthetic task: expect ~80% of true causal subspaces pass validation
steps.step4: For IOI/RAVEL: expect ~50-70% of candidate subspaces pass validation
steps.step5: Save validated atlas: results/{dataset}/validated_causal_atlas.json
steps.step6: Record validation statistics: total candidates, pass rate per check, combined pass rate

title: Compute C-GAS Scores - Synthetic Task
description: Calculate Causal Geometric Alignment Score for all methods on synthetic task with ground truth.

steps.step1: Load validated causal subspaces for synthetic task
steps.step2: For each method (SAE 1x/4x/16x, PCA 1x/4x/16x, Random 1x/4x/16x) and each seed:
steps.step3: Extract explanation features for all evaluation samples
steps.step4: Compute D_causal: pairwise cosine distance matrix in validated causal subspace space
steps.step5: Compute D_exp: pairwise cosine distance matrix in explanation feature space (top-k correlated features)
steps.step6: Compute D_full: pairwise cosine distance matrix in full activation space
steps.step7: Calculate C-GAS = ρ(D_causal, D_exp) / ρ(D_causal, D_full) where ρ = Spearman correlation

title: Compute C-GAS Scores - IOI Task
description: Calculate C-GAS for all methods on IOI task.

steps.step1: Load validated causal subspaces for IOI task (layers 8-10)
steps.step2: Same procedure as synthetic task for each method and seed
steps.step3: For each layer separately and aggregate across layers
steps.step4: Compute C-GAS per position token [IO], [S], [END]
steps.step5: Save detailed results: results/ioi/cgas_detailed.json

title: Compute C-GAS Scores - RAVEL Task
description: Calculate C-GAS for all methods on RAVEL factual recall task.

steps.step1: Load validated causal subspaces for RAVEL task (per attribute type)
steps.step2: Same procedure as previous tasks for each method and seed
steps.step3: Compute C-GAS per attribute type (country-capital, name-occupation, company-CEO)
steps.step4: Aggregate C-GAS across attribute types
steps.step5: Save detailed results: results/ravel/cgas_detailed.json

title: Ablation: C-GAS Without Validation (Illusion Detection)
description: Compare C-GAS computed with vs. without multi-method validation to demonstrate importance of safeguards.

steps.step1: Recompute C-GAS using ALL candidate subspaces (not just validated ones)
steps.step2: Compare scores: C-GAS_validated vs C-GAS_unvalidated for each method
steps.step3: Expected: C-GAS_unvalidated > C-GAS_validated but less correlated with ground truth
steps.step4: For synthetic task: compute correlation between C-GAS and true feature recovery rate for both conditions
steps.step5: Verify that validation improves predictive power of C-GAS

title: Ablation: Component Sensitivity Analysis
description: Test sensitivity of C-GAS to hyperparameters and design choices.

steps.step1: Vary distance metric: compare cosine vs Euclidean vs correlation distance
steps.step2: Vary top-k selection: k=5, 10, 20, 50 features per causal subspace
steps.step3: Vary sample size: n=50, 100, 200, 500 samples for distance matrix estimation
steps.step4: Measure C-GAS stability across these variations
steps.step5: Report coefficient of variation (std/mean) for each hyperparameter

title: Interpretability Illusion Replication
description: Replicate Makelov et al. setup to test C-GAS on illusory vs. truly causal subspaces.

steps.step1: Create synthetic circuit with parallel pathways (one active, one dormant)
steps.step2: Identify subspace via naive activation patching (may activate dormant pathway)
steps.step3: Classify as illusory (fails 2+ validation checks) vs. truly causal (passes validation)
steps.step4: Compute C-GAS separately for illusory and truly causal subspaces
steps.step5: Expected: C-GAS_illusory << C-GAS_truly_causal (validates our metric detects illusions)

title: Statistical Analysis of C-GAS Scores
description: Perform rigorous statistical analysis to test success criteria.

steps.step1: Aggregate C-GAS scores across all seeds and methods
steps.step2: Compute mean, std, 95% confidence intervals via bootstrapping (1000 samples)
steps.step3: Test Hypothesis 1 (SAEs > baselines): pairwise t-tests with Bonferroni correction
steps.step4: Test Hypothesis 2 (C-GAS predicts recovery): Pearson/Spearman correlation between C-GAS and ground-truth recovery rate
steps.step5: Test Hypothesis 3 (validation matters): paired t-test comparing C-GAS_validated vs C-GAS_unvalidated correlation with ground truth
steps.step6: Report effect sizes (Cohen's d) for all significant differences

title: Success Criteria Verification
description: Explicitly verify each success criterion from the proposal.

steps.step1: Criterion 1: Check if SAEs achieve C-GAS > 0.75 on validated subspaces
steps.step2: Perform one-tailed t-test: H0: mean_CGAS_SAE <= 0.75, H1: mean_CGAS_SAE > 0.75
steps.step3: Report p-value and conclusion
steps.step4: Criterion 2: Check if SAEs significantly higher than baselines (p < 0.01)
steps.step5: Perform ANOVA followed by post-hoc tests
steps.step6: Criterion 3: Check correlation with ground-truth recovery (r > 0.8)
steps.step7: Compute correlation and 95% CI, test H0: r <= 0.8 vs H1: r > 0.8
steps.step8: Criterion 4: Check if illusory subspaces have lower C-GAS than validated

title: Compute Additional Metrics
description: Compute supplementary metrics for comprehensive evaluation.

steps.step1: Compute reconstruction error for all methods on each dataset
steps.step2: Compute sparsity metrics: L0 (avg active features), L1 norm
steps.step3: Compute dead neuron percentage for SAEs
steps.step4: Compute explained variance for PCA
steps.step5: Compute feature interpretability via activation patterns (for qualitative validation)

title: Generate C-GAS Comparison Figure
description: Create bar plot comparing C-GAS scores across methods and datasets.

steps.step1: Load C-GAS results for all methods and datasets
steps.step2: Create grouped bar plot: x-axis = method, y-axis = C-GAS, grouped by dataset
steps.step3: Add error bars showing 95% confidence intervals
steps.step4: Add horizontal line at C-GAS = 0.75 (success threshold)
steps.step5: Add significance markers (* p<0.05, ** p<0.01, *** p<0.001) for SAE vs baseline comparisons

title: Generate Validation Impact Figure
description: Visualize impact of multi-method validation on C-GAS scores.

steps.step1: Load C-GAS with and without validation
steps.step2: Create side-by-side box plots or violin plots
steps.step3: Left plot: C-GAS distribution for validated vs unvalidated subspaces
steps.step4: Right plot: Correlation with ground truth for validated vs unvalidated
steps.step5: Add annotations showing mean difference and p-value

title: Generate Layer-wise C-GAS Heatmap
description: Create heatmap showing C-GAS across layers and methods for IOI task.

steps.step1: Load per-layer C-GAS scores for IOI task
steps.step2: Create heatmap: rows = methods, columns = layers 0-11
steps.step3: Color scale: viridis or plasma, range 0.0-1.0
steps.step4: Add annotations for known IOI circuit layers (8-10)
steps.step5: Verify C-GAS peaks at known circuit layers for good methods

title: Generate Distance Correlation Scatter Plots
description: Visualize relationship between causal and explanation distance matrices.

steps.step1: Sample 1000 distance pairs from D_causal and D_exp for best SAE
steps.step2: Create scatter plot: x = D_causal, y = D_exp
steps.step3: Add regression line and report R²
steps.step4: Create similar plot for PCA and Random baselines for comparison
steps.step5: Layout: 1×3 subplot figure

title: Generate Sensitivity Analysis Figure
description: Visualize robustness of C-GAS to hyperparameter choices.

steps.step1: Load sensitivity analysis results
steps.step2: Create 3 subplots: (1) distance metric, (2) top-k, (3) sample size
steps.step3: For each subplot: x-axis = hyperparameter value, y-axis = C-GAS mean ± std
steps.step4: Add shaded region for ±1 std
steps.step5: Indicate chosen default values with vertical dashed line

title: Generate Summary Results Table
description: Create publication-ready table with all main results.

steps.step1: Create table with columns: Method, Dataset, C-GAS Mean, C-GAS Std, vs Baseline p-value
steps.step2: Include all methods: SAE 1x/4x/16x, PCA 1x/4x/16x, Random
steps.step3: Include all datasets: Synthetic, IOI, RAVEL
steps.step4: Highlight cells where C-GAS >= 0.75 (green) and < 0.75 (red)
steps.step5: Add footnote explaining C-GAS and threshold justification

title: Compile Final Results Report
description: Generate comprehensive Markdown report summarizing all experiments.

steps.step1: Create results/FINAL_REPORT.md with sections:
steps.step2: 1. Executive Summary: hypothesis confirmation status
steps.step3: 2. Experimental Setup: models, datasets, hyperparameters
steps.step4: 3. Main Results: C-GAS comparison table and key findings
steps.step5: 4. Ablation Studies: validation impact, sensitivity analysis
steps.step6: 5. Interpretability Illusion Test: results on illusory subspaces
steps.step7: 6. Statistical Tests: all p-values and effect sizes
steps.step8: 7. Success Criteria: pass/fail for each criterion
steps.step10: Include links to all raw data files
